title: Supporting context for translation performance
prose: What supporting context — beyond the bare term — maximises EN->KO translation quality? Candidates from SNOMED itself: the semantic tag (always present, cheap disambiguation), the parent / is-a hierarchy (sense + domain), synonyms, and text definitions (rich but only ~2.4% of concepts). Plus the existing levers: retrieval exemplars and the style guide. This is the TRANSLATION-PERFORMANCE arm of the project — reduce errors at the source — distinct from the confidence thread (output-translations-confidence), which PRIORITISES review of what remains. Both serve the root goal of minimising terminologist effort, and they interact: better context lowers the error rate, and some context (e.g. detected ambiguity) may itself feed a confidence signal.

notes.body:
**Adding SNOMED concept context to the EN->KO prompt is neutral overall but helps the ambiguous tail — and ONLY if the context is fenced off.** Translated 960 concepts (120 each across 8 hierarchies, gemma4-26b-qat) under three prompts — bare / +semantic-tag / +parent hierarchy — scored vs the KR gold (multi-reference chrF + BGE semantic).

- First, a trap: naively appending '(organism)'/parent lists LEAKED into the output (땅콩(균) and HURT quality (chrF 58.0 -> 54.0). Fencing the context with explicit 'context only, do NOT include' labels fixed the leak (5/960 artifacts).
- Fenced, context is ~neutral overall: bare 58.0 / +tag 58.0 / +parents **58.5** chrF (sem ~0.82 flat).
- But it helps SELECTIVELY on ambiguous hierarchies: organism +2.8 chrF (53.2->56.0), qualifier value +3.5 (59.4->62.9); flat/slightly negative on concrete types (disorder, procedure, substance) the model already nails.

Caveats: deltas are modest and n=120/hierarchy (noisy); chrF/sem-to-gold penalises valid alternative translations, so it may UNDERSTATE context value for precision — an SME spot-check on the ambiguous tail is the real test. Text definitions (only ~2.4% of concepts) untested as a sub-condition.

Implication for prompt optimisation: don't blanket-add context — add it CONDITIONALLY for ambiguous/rare concept types, and the format must clearly separate context from the term. This is the signal to feed GEPA: let the optimiser learn WHEN to pull which context. Reproduced by scripts/analysis/concept_context_translation.py (scripted, not yet a flow node).
notes.author: research-agent
